title: Serial Dilution and Plating Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6jeb1","text":"Protocol for serial dilution and plating in the Burton Lab at Cornell University.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 990 µL, 1x PBS to each tube.
Add 10 µL, of starting culture to your 10 -2 tube. Vortex to mix.
Add 10 µL, 10^-2 to your 10 -4 tube. Vortex to mix.
Add 10 µL, 10^-4 to your 10 -6 tube. Vortex to mix.
Label each plate (LB) with the sample name and dilution (e.g., “10⁻²”, “10⁻⁴”, “10⁻⁶”).
Add 8-20 rattler beads to plate lid.
Turn plate over and then add 100 µL of cells.
Invert plates and incubate at 37 °C Overnight
Next day, get plates and count for CFUs (colony forming units). Over 300 = too numerous to count (TNTC); Under 30 = too few to count (TFTC)
creator: Aisha Burton
